description: Cross-language encoder interop test vectors for AIIR commit receipt. Any conforming implementation MUST produce identical canonical_json, content_hash, and receipt_id for each vector's input_core.

vectors.description: Minimal valid receipt — human-authored, single file, no extensions
AI-assisted commit — Copilot co-author signal detected
Unicode characters in all string fields — tests canonical JSON ensure_ascii=True encoding
vectors.implementation_notes: ensure_ascii=True means non-ASCII chars become \uXXXX escapes. All conforming implementations MUST use the same escaping.

vectors.description: Receipt with files_redacted=true and empty files array — tests empty array encoding
vectors.implementation_notes: null repository and empty files array - tests JSON null encoding and empty array handling

vectors.description: Both AI and bot flags true — tests JSON boolean encoding across languages
vectors.implementation_notes: JSON booleans: Python True/False, JS true/false, Go true/false. All MUST serialize as lowercase true/false.

vectors.description: Receipt with exactly 100 files (CDDL max) — boundary test for array encoding
vectors.implementation_notes: 100 files is the CDDL maximum per receipt.cddl. Tests array serialization with many elements.

vectors.description: Keys deliberately out of alphabetical order — tests that canonical JSON always sorts keys recursively
vectors.implementation_notes: Canonical JSON MUST use sort_keys=True (Python) or equivalent. Key order in source MUST NOT affect output.

vectors.description: Receipt with zero-valued numeric fields — tests integer 0 encoding (not false, not null)
vectors.implementation_notes: Integer 0 must encode as 0, not false or null. In JS: JSON.stringify(0) === '0', not 'false'.
